concerns.text: The acceptance scenario requires the process to complete 'within a wall-clock time budget of a predefined duration.' The term 'predefined duration' is not measurable as it lacks a specific value or a reference to where that value is defined. A test cannot verify 'predefined' without the definition.

FR-010 states the system must 'perform a warm-up verification on a -adapter subset'. The number of adapters in the subset is missing (indicated by the dash). This makes the requirement unverifiable as the scope of the warm-up is undefined.

FR-010 requires the system to 'ensure the [deferred]-adapter generation... completes within 6 hours'. While the 6-hour limit is present, the target number of adapters is marked '[deferred]'. A requirement cannot be verified if the input scale (N adapters) is unknown, as the time complexity depends on N. The spec must define the N for the verification step or the verification condition.

FR-012 requires the system to complete computation 'within a feasible time limit'. 'Feasible' is subjective and not measurable. It must be replaced with a specific duration (e.g., 6 hours) or a reference to a defined constraint.

SC-004 states execution time is measured 'against a predefined CPU-only time limit'. Similar to US-1, 'predefined' is not a measurable value. The SC must state the specific limit (e.g., 6 hours) or explicitly reference the specific requirement/section where this limit is defined.

SC-005 states peak RSS memory is measured 'against a hard limit'. The specific value of the 'hard limit' is missing. Without a number (e.g., 7168 MB), this success criterion cannot be evaluated as pass/fail.
